summary: Iteration 2 testing complete. Backend: 8/8 new pytest tests pass for /usage/month, /focus_mode (GET+PUT), goal-progress fields on /usage/today, and /coach/* (chat with real Claude Sonnet 4.5 reply, history, clear, empty-message rejection). Frontend: All 5 tabs render with proper testIDs; Stats tab shows 30-cell heatmap + best/worst day cards; Coach tab quick prompts work and produce a substantive AI reply (real Claude, not fallback) with distinct user/assistant bubbles and clear-chat-btn appearing after first message; Profile shows focus-mode card with toggle + config modal containing 24 start/end-hour pickers and silenced category chips; Dashboard correctly renders red 'OVER LIMIT' badge with '157 / 5 min' text and red border on Social Media row when goal is exceeded. One UX nit: dashboard does not auto-refresh on tab focus, so goals created from Profile won't reflect until the user pulls-to-refresh or reloads.

minor.issue: One transient 500 was logged early in the run (likely first-call race when default doc was inserted then re-fetched). All subsequent calls returned 200 with correct shape and no _id leak. Not reproducible in pytest. If it recurs, the upsert-then-refetch pattern in get_focus_mode could be replaced with a single $setOnInsert upsert that returns the new doc.

ui_bugs.issue: Dashboard fetches /usage/today only on mount; navigating to Profile to add a goal and tabbing back does not refresh the category rows, so the 'OVER LIMIT' / 'Xm GOAL' badge does not appear until the user pulls to refresh or reloads the app. Recommend adding useFocusEffect from @react-navigation/native to refetch on tab focus.

react_native_issues.issue: Same iteration-1 warnings persist: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' and 'Invalid DOM property transform-origin'. Library-level (lucide / svg). Non-blocking.

action_items: Add useFocusEffect (or pull-to-refresh) on Dashboard so goal-progress badges update after a goal is added/edited from Profile without requiring full reload.
Optional hardening: tighten /api/focus_mode GET to avoid the one observed transient 500 by combining upsert + read in a single call (or using find_one_and_update).
Iteration 1 onboarding-on-web bug (Alert.alert buttons not firing) is reportedly fixed via Platform.OS===web shortcut per request notes — not re-tested here.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~640 lines — still single-file but acceptable; will benefit from splitting into routes/usage.py, routes/coach.py, routes/focus.py once another feature is added.
All new endpoints exclude Mongo _id with explicit {"_id": 0} projection — verified via tests.
Coach chat correctly hydrates last 10 turns of history in a context-prefixed message rather than relying on session memory — robust across restarts.
Goal-progress block in get_today_usage clamps progress at 2.0 (good) and only attaches goal_* fields to categories with a goal (good — verified by test_goal_fields_attached).

All new interactive elements have testID (focus-toggle, focus-config-btn, focus-modal, start-hour-N, end-hour-N, silence-<cat>, clear-chat-btn, quick-prompt-*, msg-user, msg-assistant, monthly-heatmap, heatmap-cell-<date>, best-day-card, worst-day-card).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all flows work; goal-progress only stale on tab re-focus)

seed_data_creation: 30 days of mock usage auto-seeded by /api/usage/month and /api/usage/today on first call. No additional seed required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 features verified end-to-end against https://habit-dashboard-app-1.preview.emergentagent.com. Use /(tabs) directly to skip onboarding. To verify goal-progress on dashboard, POST /api/goals with daily_limit_minutes:5 BEFORE first dashboard mount (or reload after creating goal). Backend logged one transient 500 on the very first GET /api/focus_mode of the run; consistently 200 thereafter. AI coach uses real Claude Sonnet 4.5 — full multi-paragraph response observed. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget appears healthy in this run.
rca_of_issue: Dashboard goal badges sometimes not visible after creating a goal: app/(tabs)/index.tsx loads usage data with useEffect(...,[]) only. Expo Router tabs are persistent (do not unmount on tab switch), so the dashboard never refetches when the user returns from Profile. Mitigation: replace useEffect with useFocusEffect(useCallback(()=>{ load(); },[])) from expo-router or @react-navigation/native, OR expose a manual refresh control already present (header refresh icon on Stats) on the dashboard too.
